Task: write a tiny local comparative brief about two approaches to medical AI rollout: autonomy-first vs auditability-first. Return only 2 sentences and 2 short...
summary_text: Autonomy-first optimizes throughput and clinician reach, but in medical AI it concentrates risk where explanation, escalation, and responsibility are weakest. Auditability-first is slower, yet it preserves challengeability, traceable reasoning, and human judgment at the decision boundary, which is the safer default for high-stakes rollout. - Prefer autonomy only in low-risk, tightly bounded workflows with clear fallback paths. - Make audit logs, evidence links, and review triggers non-negotiable before scaling. consulted_worldview_source: /Users/masayuki/.claude/docs/philosophy.md used_obsidian_for_worldview: no worldview_contract_applied: true
